selected_skills.name: Docs Style Guide
selected_skills.instructionsContent:
# Docs Style Guide

- Keep examples executable and copy-paste friendly.
- Prefer short headings and direct language.
- Use bullets when they make the structure easier to scan.
- Match the repo voice instead of adding marketing fluff inside technical docs.
- When updating a README, keep the quickstart first and the caveats last.
- If a command or snippet changes, make the example line up with the CLI contract.
- Favor a small amount of context over a large wall of prose.
reasons: Skill keywords match the task text or branch.

scoredSkills.name: Code Preflight
scoredSkills.instructionsContent:
# Code Preflight

- Check scope before editing.
- Prefer the smallest possible change that still solves the task.
- Keep public interfaces explicit and stable.
- Add or update tests when behavior changes.
- Do not broaden the change set just because the agent can.
- Call out risk when a change touches config, CLI behavior, or routing logic.
- Use read-only reasoning first; write only after the preflight is clear.
